item.arguments: $fw help me shape a new audit trail feature for this repo. I know the goal but not the right UX or scope yet.
item.expected_handoff: shape runs brainstorm mode for requirements, then planning
success_definition: Treats bare $fw as the root alias for the start router.
Routes to shape as the first visible workflow stage rather than planning or work.
Explains that behavior and scope need to be clarified first, so shape should use brainstorm mode.
Preserves the path onward into planning and then work.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to help me think through a new audit trail feature for this repo. I know the goal but not the right UX or scope yet.
item.expected_handoff: shape runs brainstorm mode for requirements, then planning
success_definition: Routes to shape as the first visible workflow stage rather than planning or work.
Explains that behavior and scope need to be clarified first, so shape should use brainstorm mode.
Makes the next user input explicit instead of treating the framing as settled.
Preserves the path onward into planning and then work.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to figure out the strongest next bets for this plugin before we pick one to build.
item.expected_handoff: shape runs ideate mode for a shortlist, then brainstorm for the selected idea
success_definition: Routes to shape as the first visible workflow stage rather than brainstorm or plan.
Frames ideation as the internal shape mode because the work is not chosen yet.
Makes the next user input explicit because multiple directions are still plausible.
Keeps the selected idea flowing into brainstorming.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to help shape how this repo's development workflow should challenge users more during ideation, brainstorming, and planning before we decide what to change.
item.expected_handoff: shape runs ideate mode for ranked directions, then brainstorm for the chosen contract change
success_definition: Routes this workflow-shaping request to shape rather than jumping straight to plan or work.
Recognizes that the solution direction is not settled yet.
Makes the next user input explicit because multiple contract directions are plausible.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to research current best practices for agent-driven topic research before we decide how to build a new research helper for this plugin.
item.expected_handoff: ranked research brief, then the relevant shaping mode
success_definition: Routes direct topic investigation to research.
Treats research as a helper surface rather than a new mandatory backbone stage.
Keeps the downstream handoff into shape explicit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to refresh this repo's docs after a new CLI auth flow landed. I want a Diataxis docs pass before review.
success_definition: Routes to docs instead of work or commit.
Frames the output as a Diataxis docs update.
Keeps review as the next handoff once docs are updated.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start on docs/brainstorms/2026-04-18-audit-trail-requirements.md and get me ready to build it.
item.expected_handoff: shape runs plan mode for a reviewed technical plan, then user chooses whether to address findings, deepen, or work
success_definition: Routes to shape from an existing requirements doc and identifies plan mode as the needed internal shaping path.
Does not send the user back into brainstorming without a concrete reason.
Preserves the mandatory plan-review pass and the user choice to address findings, deepen, or work.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start on docs/plans/2026-04-18-001-feat-audit-trail-plan.md and start implementing it.
item.expected_handoff: implementation, helper checks as needed, review, commit, then offer $fw:spin when warranted
success_definition: Routes directly to work from an existing plan when implementation is requested.
Keeps review and commit as the downstream steps while allowing helper stages to stay conditional.
Includes spin as an offered end-of-session capture step when a durable lesson exists.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to help before merge. The code is already changed and I want to find bugs, regressions, or missing tests.
item.expected_handoff: review findings, then push/PR or exit
success_definition: Routes to review rather than work or shape.
Frames review as pre-merge risk finding.
Keeps the downstream path compatible with merge or PR update.

item.arguments: $flywheel the code already changed and I want a pre-merge bug-finding review before we commit.
item.expected_handoff: review findings, then push/PR or exit
success_definition: Treats bare $flywheel as the root alias for the start router, not as a separate stage namespace.
Routes to review rather than work or shape because the code has already changed.
Uses canonical fw:<stage> names for downstream stage references.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to wrap up this completed task and preserve what we learned so the next task is easier.
success_definition: Routes to spin for a completed session.
Frames spin as capture of solved problems or durable guidance.
Does not reroute back into implementation.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start for a runtime-risky queue and retry change that is coded, reviewed, and now needs safe release planning before final commit.
success_definition: Routes to rollout instead of flattening the change directly into commit.
Frames rollout as release-planning and validation work rather than deployment automation.
Keeps commit as the next handoff once rollout posture is defined.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start for a live production issue that currently has logs, metrics, and customer-impact reports but no confirmed fix path yet.
item.expected_handoff: incident brief, then debug/rollout/commit as needed
success_definition: Routes to incident handling instead of ordinary debug or work.
Frames the job as runtime-evidence intake and decision-making.
Keeps the next handoff explicit once mitigation vs rollback vs patch is chosen.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to rename the stale config key in this repo. It is a small, well-defined change and we already know the target behavior.
success_definition: Allows a direct-to-work path for a clear small change.
Does not force shaping just for ceremony.
Still preserves review, commit, and a later spin offer when it is worth keeping.

item.arguments: Use $fw:start to decide whether this repo should keep a new workflow inside the modular monolith, split a bounded context, or introduce a service boundary.
item.expected_handoff: architecture brief, then the relevant shaping or planning stage
success_definition: Routes explicit boundary and service-shape work to architecture-strategy.
Treats the helper as focused support rather than a new visible backbone stage.
Keeps the later handoff into shape, planning, or execution explicit.
